events.output: Investigated 4 areas: (1) workflow schema has no skills field, executionModel provides precedent, (2) get_skills requires activity_id, readSkill has universal fallback, (3) orchestrate-workflow dispatch and meta/rules.toon don't reference execute-activity by ID, (4) dead code: listUniversalSkills, listSkills, readSkillIndex, SkillIndex, SkillEntry, listWorkflowSkills — ~180 lines unused by server.
Not needed — problem domain is entirely internal to the workflow-server codebase.

10-file change inventory: 2 schema, 1 server, 4 workflow data, 2 dead code cleanup, 1 documentation. Key design: add optional skills[] to WorkflowSchema, make get_skills activity_id optional, update orchestrate-workflow dispatch to include get_skills(workflow_id).
1 new assumption collected: A-04-01 (workflow schema skills field should be optional).
Created 04-kb-research.md with 4 sections: schema design, API extension, meta skill updates, dead code analysis.
Updated 02-assumptions-log.md with A-04-01 (validated via code analysis).
A-04-01 validated immediately (schema uses optional fields as standard pattern). No new open assumptions. 3 original open assumptions remain (A-02-04, A-02-05, A-02-07).
Presented A-04-01 as resolved. All research-phase assumptions code-verified.

3 open stakeholder-dependent assumptions remain from prior activities: A-02-04 (scope boundary — server src changes), A-02-05 (behavioral vs enforcement), A-02-07 (trace format standardization). User's scope direction from this turn resolves A-02-04: server source changes ARE in scope (confirmed by Path 1 direction).
